other_findings.finding: T cells emerged in prenatal skin from around 10 PCW, later than innate immune cells such as macrophages and ILCs
other_findings.source_table: Fig. 1c, Extended Data Fig. 1d, Supplementary Table 4

other_findings.finding: Single-cell alpha-beta TCR sequencing was generated to accurately resolve T cell subsets
Prenatal skin matrix cells expressed CXCL14, a chemokine reported to recruit regulatory T cells, highlighting potential Treg accumulation during hair follicle matrix differentiation
Treg cells are known to localize around the hair follicle in late second trimester (around 21 PCW) and in postnatal skin

evidence_quotes.quote: Innate immune cells, such as macrophages and innate lymphoid cells (ILCs), were present from early gestation, whereas B cells and T cells emerged later, accompanying thymus, bone marrow and spleen formation from around 10 PCW
Single-cell alpha-beta T cell receptor (TCR) sequencing data were generated to accurately resolve T cell subsets
prenatal skin matrix cells had increased expression of genes involved in chemotaxis, such as CXCL14, a chemokine previously reported to recruit regulatory T (Treg) cells
evidence_quotes.context: Hair follicle matrix differentiation and Treg recruitment
